feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Persistent rain, with heavy falls and possible thunderstorms, sets in during Monday afternoon and evening, starting in the south and spreading north. Expect 80 to 120 mm to accumulate, but 150 to 250 mm about the ranges and coastal hills. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h, but 25 to 40 mm/h possible in localised places from Monday evening. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Moderate. Changes: Note, the period has been delayed by 4 hours from previous warning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, with possible thunderstorms Monday evening. Rainfall amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Note a delay in the rainfall arrival of 4 hours from previous issue.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: South to southeasterly winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Note period of strong winds extended into Tuesday evening.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 100 to 130 mm of rain, but 150 to 200 mm for the Seaward Kaikoura Range. Peak rates of 20 to 30 mm/h expected Monday morning. Freezing level lowering to 1200 to 1400 metres on Monday. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain and amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Rain is expected to turn to snow about the summit Monday afternoon, lowering to 800 metres in the evening. Peak snowfall expected Monday evening, with accumulations easing early Tuesday morning. 3 to 5cm may accumulate about and south of the summit.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow in showers is expected to lower to 800 metres Monday evening. 2 to 3 cm may accumulate on the road from Monday evening to Tuesday morning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
